95	def load_model_and_tokenizer(model_dir, device):
96	    logging.info(f"Loading model and tokenizer from {model_dir}")
97	
98	    # Determine the appropriate dtype based on the GPU capabilities
99	    def get_optimal_dtype():
100	        if not torch.cuda.is_available():
101	            logging.info("Using float32 as no GPU is available.")
102	            return torch.float32
103	
104	        device_index = torch.cuda.current_device()
105	        capability = torch.cuda.get_device_capability(device_index)
106	
107	        if capability[0] >= 8:  # sm_80 or higher
108	            logging.info("Using bfloat16 as the GPU supports sm_80 or higher.")
109	            return torch.bfloat16
110	        elif capability[0] >= 6:  # sm_60 or higher
111	            logging.info("Using float16 as the GPU supports sm_60 or higher.")
112	            return torch.float16
113	        else:
114	            logging.info("Using float32 as the GPU does not support float16 or bfloat16.")
115	            return torch.float32
116	
117	    optimal_dtype = get_optimal_dtype()
118	
119	    # Load the model with the selected dtype
120	    try:
121	        model = AutoModelForMaskedLM.from_pretrained(model_dir, trust_remote_code=True, torch_dtype=optimal_dtype)
122	        # Note: We set dtype in two places because some transformers versions are inconsistent in
123	        # honoring torch_dtype during from_pretrained. See:
124	        # https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/issues/36567
125	        # Passing torch_dtype here and also calling model.to(dtype) below ensures correctness across
126	        # versions; when from_pretrained respects torch_dtype, the subsequent .to(dtype) is a no-op.
127	        model.to(optimal_dtype)
128	    except Exception as e:
129	        logging.error(f"Failed to load model with {optimal_dtype}, falling back to float32. Error: {e}")
130	        model = AutoModelForMaskedLM.from_pretrained(model_dir, trust_remote_code=True, torch_dtype=torch.float32)
131	
132	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_dir, trust_remote_code=True)
133	    model.to(device)
134	    return model, tokenizer
135

137	def create_dataloader(sequences, tokenizer, batch_size, tokenIdx, stepSize=1, useMasking=True):
138	    logging.info(f"Creating DataLoader with batch size {batch_size}")
139	    dataset = SequenceDataset(sequences, tokenizer, tokenIdx, stepSize=stepSize, useMasking=useMasking)
140	    return DataLoader(dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False)

143	def extract_logits(model, dataloader, device, tokenIdx, tokenizer, stepSize=1, window_sizes=None):
144	    logging.info("Extracting logits")
145	    nucleotides = list('acgt')
146	    results = []
147	    
148	    window_idx = 0
149	
150	    for batch in tqdm(dataloader):
151	        curIDs = batch['input_ids'].to(device)
152	        curIDs = curIDs.squeeze(1)
153	        batch_size = curIDs.shape[0]
154	
155	        with torch.inference_mode():
156	            outputs = model(input_ids=curIDs)
157	        all_logits = outputs.logits
158	
159	        # This logic is for the windowed genome-wide LLR scoring (BED workflow)
160	        if stepSize > 1 and window_sizes is not None:
161	            start_offset = -(stepSize // 2) + (1 if stepSize % 2 == 0 else 0)
162	            for b in range(batch_size):
163	                actual_window_size = window_sizes[window_idx]
164	                window_probs = []
165	                for i in range(actual_window_size):
166	                    pos = tokenIdx + start_offset + i
167	                    logits_pos = all_logits[b, pos, [tokenizer.get_vocab()[nc] for nc in nucleotides]]
168	                    probs = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits_pos.cpu(), dim=0).numpy()
169	                    window_probs.append(probs)
170	                results.extend(window_probs)
171	                window_idx += 1
172	        # This is the generic logic for zero-shot scoring (VCF workflow)
173	        else:
174	            logits = all_logits[:, tokenIdx, [tokenizer.get_vocab()[nc] for nc in nucleotides]]
175	            probs = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits.cpu(), dim=1).numpy()
176	            results.append(probs)
177	
178	    if stepSize > 1 and window_sizes is not None:
179	        return np.array(results)
180	    else:
181	        return np.concatenate(results, axis=0)
182

184	def load_fasta(fasta_path):
185	    """Loads a FASTA file, handling .gz compression."""
186	    logging.info(f"Loading reference genome from {fasta_path}")
187	    if fasta_path.endswith(".gz"):
188	        with gzip.open(fasta_path, "rt") as file:
189	            return SeqIO.to_dict(SeqIO.parse(file, "fasta"))
190	    else:
191	        return SeqIO.to_dict(SeqIO.parse(fasta_path, "fasta"))

194	def load_bed_file(bed_path):
195	    """Load BED file and return DataFrame with chr, start, end columns."""
196	    logging.info(f"Reading BED file from {bed_path}")
197	    bed_df = pd.read_csv(bed_path, sep='\t', header=None,
198	                         names=['chr', 'start', 'end'], usecols=[0, 1, 2])
199	    return bed_df

202	def extract_sequences_from_bed(args, bed_df, fasta_dict):
203	    """Extract sequences using windowed approach for BED regions."""
204	    logging.info("Extracting sequences from BED regions with windowed approach")
205	
206	    sequences = []
207	    position_info = []  # Store chr, pos, ref_allele for each position
208	    window_sizes = []  # Track actual number of valid positions per window
209	
210	    addIdx = args.contextSize - args.tokenIdx
211	
212	    logging.info(f"Using step_size={args.stepSize}, masking={args.useMasking}")
213	
214	    for _, row in tqdm(bed_df.iterrows(), total=len(bed_df)):
215	        chrom = str(row['chr'])
216	        start = int(row['start'])
217	        end = int(row['end'])
218	
219	        if chrom not in fasta_dict:
220	            logging.warning(f"Chromosome {chrom} not found in FASTA file, skipping region")
221	            continue
222	
223	        for window_start in range(start, end, args.stepSize):
224	            window_end = min(window_start + args.stepSize, end)
225	            num_positions = window_end - window_start
226	
227	            if num_positions == 0:
228	                continue
229	
230	            center_pos = window_start + (num_positions - 1) / 2.0
231	            center_pos_int = int(center_pos)
232	
233	            try:
234	                window_refs = []
235	                window_positions = []
236	
237	                for pos in range(window_start, window_end):
238	                    ref_allele = str(fasta_dict[chrom].seq[pos]).upper()
239	                    if ref_allele not in ['A', 'C', 'G', 'T']:
240	                        continue
241	                    window_refs.append(ref_allele)
242	                    window_positions.append(pos)
243	
244	                if not window_refs:
245	                    continue
246	
247	                seq_start = center_pos_int - args.tokenIdx
248	                seq_end = center_pos_int + addIdx
249	
250	                if seq_start < 0:
251	                    seq = str(fasta_dict[chrom].seq[0:seq_end]).upper().rjust(args.contextSize, "N")
252	                else:
253	                    seq = str(fasta_dict[chrom].seq[seq_start:seq_end]).upper().ljust(args.contextSize, "N")
254	
255	                sequences.append(seq)
256	                window_sizes.append(len(window_refs))
257	
258	                for pos, ref in zip(window_positions, window_refs):
259	                    position_info.append({'chr': chrom, 'pos': pos, 'ref': ref})
260	
261	            except Exception as e:
262	                logging.warning(f"Error processing window {chrom}:{window_start}-{window_end}, skipping. Error: {e}")
263	                continue
264	
265	    logging.info(f"Extracted {len(sequences)} windows covering {len(position_info)} positions")
266	    return sequences, position_info, window_sizes
267

269	def calculate_genome_wide_llr(position_info, probs_array, aggregation):
270	    """Calculate log likelihood ratios for each position."""
271	    logging.info("Calculating genome-wide log likelihood ratios")
272	
273	    nucleotides = ['A', 'C', 'G', 'T']
274	    results = []
275	
276	    assert len(probs_array) == len(position_info), f"Mismatch: {len(probs_array)} probs vs {len(position_info)} positions"
277	
278	    for pos_info, probs in zip(position_info, probs_array):
279	        ref_allele = pos_info['ref']
280	        ref_idx = nucleotides.index(ref_allele)
281	        ref_prob = probs[ref_idx]
282	
283	        alt_indices = [i for i in range(4) if i != ref_idx]
284	        alt_probs = [probs[i] for i in alt_indices]
285	        alt_alleles = [nucleotides[i] for i in alt_indices]
286	
287	        llrs = [np.log(alt_prob / ref_prob) if ref_prob > 0 else np.inf for alt_prob in alt_probs]
288	
289	        result = {
290	            'chr': pos_info['chr'],
291	            'start': pos_info['pos'],
292	            'end': pos_info['pos'] + 1,
293	            'ref': ref_allele,
294	            'ref_prob': ref_prob,
295	            'alt_alleles': alt_alleles,
296	            'alt_probs': alt_probs,
297	            'llrs': llrs
298	        }
299	
300	        if aggregation == "max":
301	            result['score'] = max(llrs)
302	        elif aggregation == "average":
303	            result['score'] = np.mean(llrs)
304	        elif aggregation == "all":
305	            result['scores'] = llrs
306	
307	        results.append(result)
308	
309	    return results
310	
311	
312	def write_llr_output(results, output_path, aggregation, output_raw_prob):
313	    """Write genome-wide LLR results to output file."""
314	    logging.info(f"Writing LLR results to {output_path}")
315	
316	    with open(output_path, 'w') as f:
317	        if aggregation == "all":
318	            header = "chr\tstart\tend\tref\talt_alleles\tscores"
319	            if output_raw_prob: header += "\tref_prob\talt_probs"
320	        else:
321	            header = "chr\tstart\tend\tref\tscore"
322	            if output_raw_prob: header += "\tref_prob\talt_probs"
323	        f.write(header + "\n")
324	
325	        for result in results:
326	            line = f"{result['chr']}\t{result['start']}\t{result['end']}\t{result['ref']}\t"
327	            if aggregation == "all":
328	                scores_str = ','.join([f"{s:.6f}" for s in result['scores']])
329	                alt_str = ','.join(result['alt_alleles'])
330	                line += f"{alt_str}\t{scores_str}"
331	                if output_raw_prob:
332	                    alt_probs_str = ','.join([f"{p:.6f}" for p in result['alt_probs']])
333	                    line += f"\t{result['ref_prob']:.6f}\t{alt_probs_str}"
334	            else:
335	                line += f"{result['score']:.6f}"
336	                if output_raw_prob:
337	                    alt_probs_str = ','.join([f"{p:.6f}" for p in result['alt_probs']])
338	                    line += f"\t{result['ref_prob']:.6f}\t{alt_probs_str}"
339	            f.write(line + "\n")
340

342	def zero_shot_score(snpDF, logits):
343	    logging.info("Calculating zero-shot scores")
344	    nucleotides = ['A', 'C', 'G', 'T']
345	    res = []
346	    for idx, (snp, probs) in enumerate(zip(snpDF.itertuples(index=False), logits)):
347	        refAllele = getattr(snp, 'ref')
348	        altAllele = getattr(snp, 'alt')
349	        refProb = probs[nucleotides.index(refAllele)]
350	        altProb = probs[nucleotides.index(altAllele)]
351	        res.append(np.log(altProb / refProb))
352	    return res
353

355	def zero_shot_score_vcf(args, recordIndices, logits):
356	    logging.info("Calculating zero-shot scores")
357	    nucleotides = ['A', 'C', 'G', 'T']
358	
359	    reader = vcf.Reader(filename=args.inputVCF)
360	    writer = vcf.Writer(open(args.output, "w"), reader)
361	
362	    currentIdx = 0
363	
364	    rIdx = 0
365	    for record in reader:
366	        if currentIdx == recordIndices[rIdx]:
367	            scores = []
368	
369	            refAllele = record.REF
370	            refProb = logits[rIdx][nucleotides.index(refAllele)]
371	
372	            for alt in record.ALT:
373	                if alt.type == "SNV":
374	                    altAllele = alt.sequence
375	                    altProb = logits[rIdx][nucleotides.index(altAllele)]
376	                    scores.append(np.log(altProb / refProb))
377	                else:
378	                    scores.append(".")
379	
380	            record.INFO["plantCAD_zero_shot"] = ",".join(str(zs) for zs in scores)
381	
382	            writer.write_record(record)
383	
384	            rIdx += 1
385	        currentIdx += 1
386	
387	    writer.close()
388

390	def seq_from_vcf(args):
391	    logging.info(f"Reading input data from {args.inputVCF}")
392	
393	    fastaDict = load_fasta(args.inputFasta)
394	
395	    sequences = []
396	    recordIndices = []  # keep track of which record a sequence belongs to
397	    vcfReader = vcf.Reader(filename=args.inputVCF)
398	    recordIdx = 0
399	    prevChrom = ""
400	    addIdx = args.contextSize - args.tokenIdx
401	
402	    for record in vcfReader:
403	        if any([alt.type == "SNV" for alt in record.ALT]):
404	            chrom = record.CHROM
405	            pos = record.POS - 1  # convert to zero-based indexing
406	
407	            try:
408	                if pos - args.tokenIdx < 0:
409	                    seq = str(fastaDict[chrom][0:pos + addIdx].seq).upper().rjust(args.contextSize, "N")
410	                else:
411	                    seq = str(fastaDict[chrom][pos - args.tokenIdx:pos + addIdx].seq).upper().ljust(args.contextSize, "N")
412	
413	                sequences.append(seq)
414	                recordIndices.append(recordIdx)
415	
416	                # RAM saving: remove chromosomes when the corresponding vcf records have been processed
417	                if prevChrom != chrom:
418	                    if prevChrom != "":
419	                        fastaDict.pop(prevChrom)
420	                    prevChrom = chrom
421	            except:
422	                print("Error processing VCF at record " + str(recordIdx))
423	                print("Check that VCF file is sorted and chromosome names match FASTA file.")
424	                print(record)
425	                sys.exit()
426	        recordIdx += 1
427	    return sequences, recordIndices

430	def main():
431	    logging.basicConfig(
432	        level=logging.INFO,
433	        format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s',
434	        datefmt='%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'
435	    )
436	    args = parse_args()
437	
438	    # ----- Enforce model-specific context size constraints -----
439	    model_name_lower = args.model.lower()
440	    if "plantcad2" in model_name_lower:
441	        MIN_CTX = 2048
442	        if args.contextSize < MIN_CTX:
443	            logging.warning(
444	                f"PlantCAD2 model detected. Specified contextSize={args.contextSize} is below "
445	                f"the recommended minimum of {MIN_CTX}. Forcing contextSize={MIN_CTX}."
446	            )
447	            args.contextSize = MIN_CTX
448	            args.tokenIdx = args.contextSize // 2 - 1
449	    elif "plantcaduceus" in model_name_lower:
450	        FIXED_CTX = 512
451	        if args.contextSize != FIXED_CTX:
452	            logging.warning(
453	                f"PlantCaduceus (PlantCAD v1) model detected. Forcing contextSize={FIXED_CTX} "
454	                f"(was {args.contextSize})."
455	            )
456	            args.contextSize = FIXED_CTX
457	            args.tokenIdx = args.contextSize // 2 - 1
458	
459	    # Determine workflow based on input type
460	    if args.inputVCF is not None:
461	        # VCF-based zero-shot scoring workflow
462	        sequences, recordIndices = seq_from_vcf(args)
463	
464	        model, tokenizer = load_model_and_tokenizer(args.model, args.device)
465	
466	        logging.info("Creating data loader")
467	        loader = create_dataloader(sequences, tokenizer, args.batchSize, args.tokenIdx)
468	
469	        logits = extract_logits(model, loader, args.device, args.tokenIdx, tokenizer)
470	
471	        zero_shot_score_vcf(args, recordIndices, logits)
472	        logging.info(f"Zero-shot scores saved to {args.output}")
473	
474	    elif args.inputBed is not None:
475	        # BED-based genome-wide LLR scoring workflow
476	        logging.info("Starting genome-wide LLR scoring workflow.")
477	        
478	        # Load reference genome and BED file
479	        fasta_dict = load_fasta(args.inputFasta)
480	        bed_df = load_bed_file(args.inputBed)
481	
482	        # Extract sequences for all positions in BED regions
483	        sequences, position_info, window_sizes = extract_sequences_from_bed(args, bed_df, fasta_dict)
484	
485	        if not sequences:
486	            logging.error("No valid sequences extracted. Check BED file and FASTA file.")
487	            sys.exit(1)
488	
489	        # Load model and tokenizer
490	        model, tokenizer = load_model_and_tokenizer(args.model, args.device)
491	
492	        # Create dataloader with BED-specific settings
493	        loader = create_dataloader(sequences, tokenizer, args.batchSize, args.tokenIdx,
494	                                   args.stepSize, args.useMasking)
495	
496	        # Extract logits/probabilities
497	        probs = extract_logits(model, loader, args.device, args.tokenIdx, tokenizer, 
498	                               args.stepSize, window_sizes)
499	
500	        # Calculate LLRs
501	        results = calculate_genome_wide_llr(position_info, probs, args.aggregation)
502	
503	        # Write output
504	        write_llr_output(results, args.output, args.aggregation, args.outputRawProb)
505	
506	        logging.info(f"Genome-wide LLR calculation complete. Results saved to {args.output}")
507	
508	    else:
509	        logging.error("No input file specified. Please use -input-vcf or -input-bed.")
510	        sys.exit(1)
511

514	    main()

39	# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	# Tokenization
41	# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	
43	def tokenize(
44	    data_dir: Optional[str] = None,
45	    output_path: Optional[str] = None,
46	    model_name: Optional[str] = None,
47	    sequence_length: int = 8192,
48	    batch_size: int = 1000,
49	    max_batches: Optional[int] = None,
50	    num_proc: Optional[int] = None,
51	    task_type: str = "classification",
52	    # HF dataset options
53	    hf_dataset: Optional[str] = None,
54	    hf_config: Optional[str] = None,
55	    hf_split: str = "train",
56	    # Column names
57	    seq_column: str = "sequence",
58	    label_column: str = "label",
59	) -> None:
60	    """Tokenize sequences from a local TSV or a Hugging Face dataset and save to parquet.
61	
62	    Inputs
63	    - local TSV: pass data_dir=path/to/file.tsv (expects columns `Sequence`, `Label`; case-insensitive)
64	    - HF dataset: pass hf_dataset='namespace/dataset' and optionally hf_config, hf_split
65	
66	    Labels
67	    - multi_label: Label can be a string of 0/1 (e.g., '0101...') or a list[int]
68	    - classification/regression: Label should be numeric
69	    """
70	    if model_name is None:
71	        raise ValueError("model_name must be provided to load the tokenizer")
72	    if data_dir is None and hf_dataset is None:
73	        raise ValueError("Provide either data_dir (local TSV) or hf_dataset (Hugging Face)")
74	
75	    if num_proc is None:
76	        num_proc = multiprocessing.cpu_count()
77	
78	    logger.info(f"Loading tokenizer from {model_name}")
79	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name, trust_remote_code=True)
80	
81	    # Load dataset from source
82	    if hf_dataset is not None:
83	        logger.info(f"Loading HF dataset: {hf_dataset} (config={hf_config}, split={hf_split})")
84	        dataset = load_dataset(hf_dataset, hf_config, split=hf_split)
85	    else:
86	        if output_path is None:
87	            output_path = str(Path(data_dir).with_suffix(".parquet"))
88	        logger.info(f"Loading local TSV: {data_dir}")
89	        if task_type == "multi_label":
90	            dtype = {label_column: str, label_column.capitalize(): str, label_column.upper(): str}
91	            df = pd.read_csv(data_dir, sep="\t", dtype=dtype)
92	            dataset = Dataset.from_pandas(df, preserve_index=False)
93	        else:
94	            dataset = Dataset.from_csv(data_dir, sep="\t")
95	
96	    # normalize column names to lowercase
97	    for c in list(dataset.column_names):
98	        new_name = c.lower()
99	        if c != new_name:
100	            dataset = dataset.rename_column(c, new_name)
101	    # also normalize provided column names
102	    seq_column = seq_column.lower()
103	    label_column = label_column.lower()
104	
105	    # optionally subsample for quick tests
106	    if max_batches is not None:
107	        max_examples = max_batches * batch_size
108	        dataset = dataset.select(range(min(max_examples, len(dataset))))
109	        logger.info(f"Limited dataset to {max_examples} examples ({max_batches} batches)")
110	
111	    # Validate that all sequences match the specified sequence_length
112	    logger.info(f"Validating that all sequences are exactly {sequence_length} characters long...")
113	    seq_lengths = set(len(str(seq)) for seq in dataset[seq_column])
114	    if seq_lengths != {sequence_length}:
115	        raise SystemExit(
116	            f"ERROR: Not all sequences are {sequence_length} characters long.\n"
117	            f"  Found sequence lengths: {sorted(seq_lengths)}\n"
118	            f"  Please specify the correct --sequence_length to match your data."
119	        )
120	
121	    def _to_label_list(val):
122	        if isinstance(val, str):
123	            return [int(c) for c in val]
124	        if isinstance(val, (list, tuple, np.ndarray)):
125	            return [int(x) for x in val]
126	        # some datasets may store as bytes; try decode
127	        try:
128	            s = str(val)
129	            return [int(c) for c in s]
130	        except Exception:
131	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported multi_label value type: {type(val)} -> {val}")
132	
133	    def tokenize_batch(examples):
134	        if seq_column not in examples:
135	            raise KeyError(f"Missing sequence column '{seq_column}' in dataset; set --seq_column if different")
136	        tokenized = tokenizer(
137	            [str(seq) for seq in examples[seq_column]],
138	            padding="max_length",
139	            truncation=True,
140	            max_length=sequence_length,
141	            add_special_tokens=False,
142	        )
143	
144	        # sanity check: all sequences should be equal length after tokenization
145	        lengths = set(map(len, tokenized["input_ids"]))
146	        if lengths != {sequence_length}:
147	            raise ValueError(
148	                f"All sequences must be of length {sequence_length}; found batch lengths {lengths}"
149	            )
150	
151	        result = {"input_ids": tokenized["input_ids"]}
152	        if task_type == "multi_label":
153	            if label_column not in examples:
154	                raise KeyError(f"Missing label column '{label_column}' for multi_label tasks")
155	            label_list = [_to_label_list(label) for label in examples[label_column]]
156	            result["labels"] = label_list
157	            remove_cols = [seq_column, label_column]
158	        else:
159	            if label_column in examples:
160	                result["label"] = examples[label_column]
161	            remove_cols = [seq_column]
162	        return result
163	
164	    logger.info(f"Tokenizing sequences in batches of {batch_size}")
165	    tokenized_dataset = dataset.map(
166	        tokenize_batch,
167	        batched=True,
168	        batch_size=batch_size,
169	        remove_columns=[c for c in [seq_column, label_column] if c in dataset.column_names],
170	        num_proc=num_proc,
171	    )
172	
173	    if output_path is None:
174	        # when hf_dataset used and output not provided, build a default name
175	        if hf_dataset is not None:
176	            src_name = hf_dataset.replace("/", "_")
177	            cfg = f"_{hf_config}" if hf_config else ""
178	            output_path = f"{src_name}{cfg}_{hf_split}_tokenized.parquet"
179	        else:
180	            src_name = Path(data_dir).stem
181	            output_path = f"{src_name}_{hf_split}_tokenized.parquet"
182	
183	    logger.info(f"Saving tokenized dataset to {output_path}")
184	    tokenized_dataset.to_parquet(output_path, compression="zstd")
185	    logger.info("Tokenization complete")
186

191	
192	def display(
193	    model_name: str,
194	    task_type: str = "classification",
195	    num_labels: Optional[int] = None,
196	) -> None:
197	    """Display a LoRA-adapted model structure and parameter trainability.
198	
199	    Parameters
200	    - model_name: HF model id or path
201	    - task_type: classification | regression | multi_label (default: classification)
202	    - num_labels: required for multi_label
203	    """
204	    if task_type not in {"classification", "regression", "multi_label"}:
205	        raise ValueError("task_type must be one of {'classification','regression','multi_label'}")
206	    if task_type == "multi_label" and (num_labels is None or num_labels <= 1):
207	        raise ValueError("For multi_label, please provide num_labels > 1")
208	
209	    logger.info(f"Loading base model from {model_name} for task_type={task_type}")
210	    base_model = load_base_model(model_name=model_name, task_type=task_type, num_labels=num_labels if task_type == "multi_label" else num_labels)
211	
212	    logger.info("Configuring LoRA adapter")
213	    model = create_peft_model(base_model)
214	
215	    logger.info(f"Model structure:\n{model}")
216	    logger.info("Model parameters:")
217	    parameters = []
218	    for n, p in model.named_parameters():
219	        parameters.append(
220	            dict(
221	                name=n,
222	                is_trainable=p.requires_grad,
223	                shape=tuple(p.shape) if hasattr(p, "shape") else None,
224	                size=p.numel(),
225	            )
226	        )
227	
228	    # pretty log
229	    col_widths = {
230	        "name": max(len("Name"), max(len(str(p["name"])) for p in parameters)),
231	        "is_trainable": max(len("Trainable"), max(len(str(p["is_trainable"])) for p in parameters)),
232	        "shape": max(len("Shape"), max(len(str(p["shape"])) for p in parameters)),
233	        "size": max(len("Size"), max(len(str(p["size"])) for p in parameters)),
234	    }
235	    col_widths = {k: v + 2 for k, v in col_widths.items()}
236	    header = (
237	        f"{'Name':<{col_widths['name']}} "
238	        f"{'Trainable':<{col_widths['is_trainable']}} "
239	        f"{'Shape':<{col_widths['shape']}} "
240	        f"{'Size':<{col_widths['size']}}"
241	    )
242	    logger.info(header)
243	    logger.info("-" * (sum(col_widths.values()) + len(col_widths) - 1))
244	    for param in parameters:
245	        row = (
246	            f"{param['name']:<{col_widths['name']}} "
247	            f"{str(param['is_trainable']):<{col_widths['is_trainable']}} "
248	            f"{str(param['shape']):<{col_widths['shape']}} "
249	            f"{param['size']:<{col_widths['size']}}"
250	        )
251	        logger.info(row)
252

255	# Train / Evaluate / Predict
256	# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
257	
258	def train(
259	    train_dir: str,
260	    valid_dir: str,
261	    output_dir: str = "/tmp/pcv2-ft",
262	    model_name: Optional[str] = None,
263	    task_type: str = "classification",  # classification | regression | multi_label
264	    num_labels: Optional[int] = None,    # required for multi_label
265	    train_batch_size: int = 43,
266	    eval_batch_size: int = 3,
267	    eval_num_samples: Optional[int] = 0,
268	    max_steps: int = 500,
269	    seed: int = 42,
270	    use_wandb: bool = False,
271	    wandb_project: str = "pcv2-ft",
272	    wandb_run_name: Optional[str] = None,
273	    learning_rate: float = 1e-3,
274	    warmup_steps: int = 50,
275	    lr_scheduler_type: str = "linear",
276	    gradient_accumulation_steps: int = 64,
277	    bf16: bool = True,
278	    num_train_epochs: int = 3,
279	    weight_decay: float = 0.01,
280	    eval_strategy: str = "steps",
281	    eval_steps: int = 25,
282	    save_strategy: str = "steps",
283	    save_steps: int = 100,
284	    logging_steps: int = 100,
285	    remove_unused_columns: bool = False,
286	    resume_from_checkpoint: Optional[str] = None,
287	) -> None:
288	    """Fine-tune PlantCAD2 with LoRA across multiple task types."""
289	    if model_name is None:
290	        raise ValueError("model_name is required")
291	
292	    if task_type not in {"classification", "regression", "multi_label"}:
293	        raise ValueError("task_type must be one of {'classification','regression','multi_label'}")
294	
295	    if task_type == "multi_label" and (num_labels is None or num_labels <= 1):
296	        raise ValueError("For multi_label, please provide num_labels > 1")
297	
298	    logger.info(f"Loading base model from {model_name}")
299	    base_model = load_base_model(model_name=model_name, task_type=task_type, num_labels=num_labels)
300	
301	    logger.info("Configuring LoRA adapter")
302	    model = create_peft_model(base_model)
303	
304	    trainable_params, all_params = model.get_nb_trainable_parameters()
305	    logger.info("Trainable parameters: %d", trainable_params)
306	    logger.info("All parameters: %d", all_params)
307	    logger.info("Percent trainable: %.2f%%", trainable_params / all_params * 100)
308	
309	    # Load datasets
310	    logger.info("Loading datasets")
311	    train_dataset = Dataset.from_parquet(str(Path(train_dir)), keep_in_memory=False)
312	    eval_dataset = Dataset.from_parquet(str(Path(valid_dir)), keep_in_memory=False)
313	
314	    # For multi_label: ensure labels are stored as 'labels' column with numeric lists.
315	    # Handles: string labels ("000110101"), list labels, or renamed columns.
316	    if task_type == "multi_label":
317	        for name, ds in [("train", train_dataset), ("eval", eval_dataset)]:
318	            # Find the label column
319	            if "labels" in ds.column_names:
320	                lbl_col = "labels"
321	            elif "label" in ds.column_names:
322	                lbl_col = "label"
323	            else:
324	                raise ValueError(f"No 'label' or 'labels' column found in {name} dataset")
325	
326	            # Check actual data format from first sample
327	            sample_label = ds[0][lbl_col]
328	            if isinstance(sample_label, str):
329	                logger.info(f"Converting string labels to numeric lists in {name} dataset")
330	                def _convert_str(examples, col=lbl_col):
331	                    return {"labels": [[int(c) for c in str(v)] for v in examples[col]]}
332	                remove = [lbl_col] if lbl_col != "labels" else []
333	                ds = ds.map(_convert_str, batched=True, remove_columns=remove)
334	            elif isinstance(sample_label, (list, np.ndarray)):
335	                if lbl_col != "labels":
336	                    ds = ds.rename_column(lbl_col, "labels")
337	                logger.info(f"Labels in {name} dataset are already lists (len={len(sample_label)})")
338	            elif isinstance(sample_label, (int, np.integer)):
339	                if num_labels is None:
340	                    raise ValueError(
341	                        f"Labels in {name} are scalars (type=int) — likely multi-label strings "
342	                        f"parsed as integers. Provide --num_labels so we can zero-pad them back."
343	                    )
344	                logger.info(
345	                    f"Converting int labels to {num_labels}-digit zero-padded binary vectors "
346	                    f"in {name} dataset (e.g. int 0 -> '{'0' * num_labels}' -> [0]*{num_labels})"
347	                )
348	                def _convert_int(examples, col=lbl_col, nl=num_labels):
349	                    return {"labels": [[int(c) for c in str(v).zfill(nl)] for v in examples[col]]}
350	                remove = [lbl_col] if lbl_col != "labels" else []
351	                ds = ds.map(_convert_int, batched=True, remove_columns=remove)
352	            else:
353	                raise ValueError(
354	                    f"Labels in {name} dataset have unsupported type ({type(sample_label).__name__}). "
355	                    f"Expected string, list of ints, or int (zero-padded multi-label)."
356	                )
357	
358	            # Verify label dimension matches num_labels
359	            verified_sample = ds[0]["labels"]
360	            sample_len = len(verified_sample) if isinstance(verified_sample, (list, np.ndarray)) else None
361	            if sample_len is not None and num_labels is not None and sample_len != num_labels:
362	                raise ValueError(
363	                    f"Label length mismatch in {name}: each label has {sample_len} elements "
364	                    f"but num_labels={num_labels}"
365	                )
366	
367	            if name == "train":
368	                train_dataset = ds
369	            else:
370	                eval_dataset = ds
371	
372	    # Remove metadata columns the collator can't handle
373	    for name, ds in [("train", train_dataset), ("eval", eval_dataset)]:
374	        extra_cols = [c for c in ds.column_names if c not in {"input_ids", "label", "labels", "attention_mask"}]
375	        if extra_cols:
376	            logger.info(f"Removing extra columns from {name} dataset: {extra_cols}")
377	            ds = ds.remove_columns(extra_cols)
378	            if name == "train":
379	                train_dataset = ds
380	            else:
381	                eval_dataset = ds
382	
383	
384	    logger.info(f"Train dataset: {train_dataset}")
385	    logger.info(f"Eval dataset: {eval_dataset}")
386	
387	    if eval_num_samples:
388	        logger.info(f"Limiting eval dataset to {eval_num_samples} samples")
389	        eval_dataset = eval_dataset.select(range(min(eval_num_samples, len(eval_dataset))))
390	
391	    if use_wandb:
392	        os.environ["WANDB_PROJECT"] = wandb_project
393	
394	    training_args = TrainingArguments(
395	        output_dir=output_dir,
396	        learning_rate=learning_rate,
397	        warmup_steps=warmup_steps,
398	        lr_scheduler_type=lr_scheduler_type,
399	        max_steps=max_steps,
400	        gradient_accumulation_steps=gradient_accumulation_steps,
401	        per_device_train_batch_size=train_batch_size,
402	        per_device_eval_batch_size=eval_batch_size,
403	        bf16=bf16,
404	        num_train_epochs=num_train_epochs,
405	        weight_decay=weight_decay,
406	        eval_strategy=eval_strategy,
407	        eval_steps=eval_steps,
408	        save_strategy=save_strategy,
409	        save_steps=save_steps,
410	        logging_steps=logging_steps,
411	        report_to="none" if not use_wandb else "wandb",
412	        run_name=wandb_run_name,
413	        remove_unused_columns=remove_unused_columns,
414	        save_total_limit=5,
415	        seed=seed,
416	    )
417	
418	    compute = (
419	        compute_metrics_multilabel
420	        if task_type == "multi_label"
421	        else (compute_metrics_classification if task_type == "classification" else compute_metrics_regression)
422	    )
423	
424	    trainer = Trainer(
425	        model=model,
426	        args=training_args,
427	        train_dataset=train_dataset,
428	        eval_dataset=eval_dataset,
429	        compute_metrics=compute,
430	    )
431	
432	    if resume_from_checkpoint is not None:
433	        logger.info(f"Resuming training from checkpoint: {resume_from_checkpoint}")
434	        trainer.train(resume_from_checkpoint=resume_from_checkpoint)
435	    else:
436	        trainer.train()
437	
438	
439	def evaluate(
440	    checkpoint_dir: str,
441	    data_dir: str,
442	    output_dir: str = "/tmp/pcv2-ft-eval",
443	    model_name: Optional[str] = None,
444	    task_type: str = "classification",
445	    num_labels: Optional[int] = None,
446	    batch_size: int = 32,
447	    bf16: bool = True,
448	    sampling_rate: Optional[float] = None,
449	    seed: int = 42,
450	) -> None:
451	    """Evaluate a LoRA checkpoint (local dir or HF repo) on validation data."""
452	
453	    if task_type == "multi_label" and (num_labels is None or num_labels <= 1):
454	        raise ValueError("For multi_label, please provide num_labels > 1")
455	
456	    checkpoint_path = Path(checkpoint_dir)
457	    if checkpoint_path.exists():
458	        if model_name is None:
459	            raise ValueError("model_name is required when loading a local checkpoint directory")
460	        logger.info(f"Loading local LoRA checkpoint from: {checkpoint_dir}")
461	        base_model = load_base_model(model_name=model_name, task_type=task_type, num_labels=num_labels)
462	        model = PeftModel.from_pretrained(base_model, checkpoint_dir)
463	    else:
464	        logger.info(f"Loading LoRA adapter from Hugging Face: {checkpoint_dir}")
465	        model = load_model_from_hf(checkpoint_dir, task_type=task_type, num_labels=num_labels)
466	
467	    logger.info(f"Loading dataset from {data_dir}")
468	    eval_dataset = Dataset.from_parquet(data_dir, keep_in_memory=False)
469	
470	    
471	
472	    if sampling_rate:
473	        if sampling_rate > 1 or sampling_rate <= 0:
474	            raise ValueError("sampling_rate must be in (0, 1]")
475	        max_index = min(int(sampling_rate * len(eval_dataset)), len(eval_dataset))
476	        max_index = max(max_index, 1)
477	        eval_dataset = eval_dataset.shuffle(seed=seed).select(range(max_index))
478	
479	    trainer_args = TrainingArguments(
480	        output_dir=output_dir,
481	        per_device_eval_batch_size=batch_size,
482	        seed=seed,
483	        report_to="none",
484	        remove_unused_columns=False,
485	        bf16=bf16,
486	    )
487	    compute = (
488	        compute_metrics_multilabel
489	        if task_type == "multi_label"
490	        else (compute_metrics_classification if task_type == "classification" else compute_metrics_regression)
491	    )
492	    trainer = Trainer(model=model, args=trainer_args, compute_metrics=compute)
493	
494	    logger.info("Evaluating model...")
495	    results = trainer.evaluate(eval_dataset=eval_dataset)
496	    logger.info("Evaluation complete")
497	    logger.info(f"Results:\n{results}")
498	
499	
500	def predict(
501	    checkpoint_dir: str,
502	    data_dir: str,
503	    output_file: str = "/tmp/predictions.csv",
504	    model_name: Optional[str] = None,
505	    task_type: str = "classification",
506	    bf16: bool = True,
507	    num_labels: Optional[int] = None,
508	    batch_size: int = 32,
509	    sampling_rate: Optional[float] = None,
510	    seed: int = 42,
511	) -> None:
512	    """Generate predictions and save to CSV.
513	
514	    - classification: outputs probability of positive class in column 'probability_positive'.
515	    - regression: outputs predicted_value.
516	    - multi_label: outputs one probability column per class: class_0, class_1, ...
517	    """
518	    if task_type == "multi_label" and (num_labels is None or num_labels <= 1):
519	        raise ValueError("For multi_label, please provide num_labels > 1")
520	
521	    # Determine whether checkpoint_dir is a local path or a HF repo id
522	    checkpoint_path = Path(checkpoint_dir)
523	    if checkpoint_path.exists():
524	        if model_name is None:
525	            raise ValueError("model_name is required when loading a local checkpoint directory")
526	        logger.info(f"Loading local LoRA checkpoint from: {checkpoint_dir}")
527	        base_model = load_base_model(model_name=model_name, task_type=task_type, num_labels=num_labels)
528	        model = PeftModel.from_pretrained(base_model, checkpoint_dir)
529	    else:
530	        logger.info(f"Loading LoRA adapter from Hugging Face: {checkpoint_dir}")
531	        model = load_model_from_hf(checkpoint_dir, task_type=task_type, num_labels=num_labels)
532	
533	    logger.info(f"Loading dataset from {data_dir}")
534	    dataset = Dataset.from_parquet(data_dir, keep_in_memory=False)
535	
536	    # For prediction, drop any label columns to avoid collator errors
537	    cols_to_keep = {"input_ids", "attention_mask"}
538	    cols_to_remove = [c for c in dataset.column_names if c not in cols_to_keep]
539	    if cols_to_remove:
540	        dataset = dataset.remove_columns(cols_to_remove)
541	    if "input_ids" not in dataset.column_names:
542	        raise ValueError(
543	            "Dataset must contain 'input_ids'. Tokenize your data first via `tokenize` and pass the resulting parquet."
544	        )
545	
546	    
547	
548	    if sampling_rate:
549	        if sampling_rate > 1 or sampling_rate <= 0:
550	            raise ValueError("sampling_rate must be in (0, 1]")
551	        max_index = min(int(sampling_rate * len(dataset)), len(dataset))
552	        max_index = max(max_index, 1)
553	        dataset = dataset.shuffle(seed=seed).select(range(max_index))
554	
555	    trainer_args = TrainingArguments(
556	        output_dir="/tmp",
557	        per_device_eval_batch_size=batch_size,
558	        seed=seed,
559	        report_to="none",
560	        remove_unused_columns=False,
561	        bf16=bf16,
562	    )
563	    trainer = Trainer(model=model, args=trainer_args)
564	
565	    logger.info("Generating predictions...")
566	    predictions = trainer.predict(test_dataset=dataset).predictions
567	
568	    if task_type == "classification":
569	        probs = torch.nn.functional.softmax(torch.tensor(predictions), dim=1).numpy()
570	        scores = probs[:, 1]
571	        df = pd.DataFrame({"probability_positive": scores})
572	    elif task_type == "regression":
573	        values = predictions.squeeze()
574	        df = pd.DataFrame({"predicted_value": values})
575	    else:  # multi_label
576	        probs = torch.sigmoid(torch.tensor(predictions)).numpy()
577	        df = pd.DataFrame(probs, columns=[f"class_{i}" for i in range(probs.shape[1])])
578	
579	    output_path = Path(output_file)
580	    logger.info(f"Saving predictions to {output_path}")
581	    df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)
582	    logger.info("Prediction scores saved successfully")
583	
584	
585	# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
586	# Helpers
587	# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
588	
589	def load_model_from_hf(hf_model_path: str, task_type: str, num_labels: Optional[int]):
590	    """Load a PEFT LoRA adapter hosted on Hugging Face by repo id or path.
591	
592	    This resolves the correct base model from the adapter's PEFT config and
593	    constructs the base with the appropriate head for the specified task.
594	    """
595	    config = PeftConfig.from_pretrained(hf_model_path)
596	    base_model = load_base_model(
597	        model_name=config.base_model_name_or_path,
598	        task_type=task_type,
599	        num_labels=num_labels,
600	    )
601	    model = PeftModel.from_pretrained(base_model, hf_model_path)
602	    return model
603	
604	def compute_metrics_classification(eval_pred):
605	    predictions, labels = eval_pred
606	    probs = torch.nn.functional.softmax(torch.tensor(predictions), dim=1)
607	    preds = np.argmax(predictions, axis=1)
608	    scores = probs[:, 1].numpy()
609	
610	    balance = np.sum(labels) / len(labels)
611	    return {
612	        "accuracy": accuracy_score(labels, preds),
613	        "f1": f1_score(labels, preds),
614	        "roc_auc": roc_auc_score(labels, scores),
615	        "average_precision": average_precision_score(labels, scores),
616	        "balance": balance,
617	    }
618	
619	
620	def compute_metrics_regression(eval_pred):
621	    predictions, labels = eval_pred
622	    predictions = predictions.squeeze()
623	    mse = ((predictions - labels) ** 2).mean()
624	    rmse = np.sqrt(mse)
625	    mae = np.abs(predictions - labels).mean()
626	    ss_tot = ((labels - labels.mean()) ** 2).sum()
627	    ss_res = ((labels - predictions) ** 2).sum()
628	    r2 = 1 - (ss_res / (ss_tot + 1e-8))
629	    pearson_corr, _ = pearsonr(predictions, labels)
630	    spearman_corr, _ = spearmanr(predictions, labels)
631	    return {
632	        "mse": mse,
633	        "rmse": rmse,
634	        "mae": mae,
635	        "r2": r2,
636	        "pearson_r": pearson_corr,
637	        "spearman_r": spearman_corr,
638	    }
639	
640	
641	def compute_metrics_multilabel(eval_pred):
642	    predictions, labels = eval_pred
643	    probs = torch.sigmoid(torch.tensor(predictions)).numpy()
644	    preds = (probs > 0.5).astype(int)
645	    return {
646	        "accuracy": accuracy_score(labels, preds),
647	        "f1": f1_score(labels, preds, average="micro"),
648	        "roc_auc": roc_auc_score(labels, probs, average="micro"),
649	        "average_precision": average_precision_score(labels, probs, average="micro"),
650	    }
651	
652	
653	def load_base_model(model_name: str, task_type: str, num_labels: Optional[int]) -> AutoModelForSequenceClassification:
654	    if task_type == "classification":
655	        base_model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(
656	            model_name,
657	            trust_remote_code=True,
658	            num_labels=2,
659	            id2label={0: "NEGATIVE", 1: "POSITIVE"},
660	            label2id={"NEGATIVE": 0, "POSITIVE": 1},
661	        )
662	    elif task_type == "regression":
663	        base_model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(
664	            model_name,
665	            trust_remote_code=True,
666	            num_labels=1,
667	            problem_type="regression",
668	        )
669	    else:  # multi_label
670	        if num_labels is None:
671	            raise ValueError("num_labels is required for multi_label classification")
672	        base_model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(
673	            model_name,
674	            trust_remote_code=True,
675	            num_labels=num_labels,
676	            problem_type="multi_label_classification",
677	        )
678	
679	    # Wrap forward to pass explicit kwargs HF expects
680	    original_forward = base_model.forward
681	
682	    def forward_with_labels(*args, **kwargs):
683	        # ensure 'labels' key exists and typed correctly for multi-label
684	        labels = kwargs.get("labels", kwargs.get("label"))
685	        if labels is None:
686	            return original_forward(input_ids=kwargs["input_ids"])  # inference without labels
687	        if task_type == "multi_label":
688	            labels = labels.float()
689	            if labels.dim() == 1:
690	                raise ValueError(
691	                    f"Multi-label labels have wrong shape {tuple(labels.shape)}. "
692	                    f"Expected [batch_size, {num_labels}]. "
693	                    f"Ensure your dataset's label column contains lists of ints, not scalars."
694	                )
695	        return original_forward(input_ids=kwargs["input_ids"], labels=labels)
696	
697	    base_model.forward = forward_with_labels
698	    return base_model
699	
700	
701	def create_peft_model(base_model: AutoModelForSequenceClassification) -> PeftModelForSequenceClassification:
702	    peft_config = LoraConfig(
703	        task_type=TaskType.SEQ_CLS,
704	        inference_mode=False,
705	        r=8,
706	        lora_alpha=32,
707	        lora_dropout=0.1,
708	        target_modules=["x_proj", "in_proj", "out_proj"],
709	    )
710	    return get_peft_model(base_model, peft_config)
711	
712	
713	# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
714	# Main
715	# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
716	
717	if __name__ == "__main__":
718	    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
719	    fire.Fire(
720	        {
721	            "tokenize": tokenize,
722	            "train": train,
723	            "evaluate": evaluate,
724	            "predict": predict,
725	            "display": display,
726	        }
727	    )

58	class DataCollatorForLanguageModelingSimplified(DataCollatorForLanguageModeling):
59	    # Simplified to skip padding since we'll assume all sequences have the same length
60	    def torch_call(
61	        self, examples: List[Union[List[int], Any, Dict[str, Any]]]
62	    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
63	        batch = {
64	            key: torch.stack(
65	                [torch.tensor(example[key]) for example in examples], dim=0
66	            )
67	            for key in examples[0].keys()
68	        }
69	
70	        # If special token mask has been preprocessed, pop it from the dict.
71	        special_tokens_mask = batch.pop("special_tokens_mask", None)
72	        if self.mlm:
73	            batch["input_ids"], batch["labels"] = self.torch_mask_tokens(
74	                batch["input_ids"], special_tokens_mask=special_tokens_mask
75	            )
76	        else:
77	            labels = batch["input_ids"].clone()
78	            if self.tokenizer.pad_token_id is not None:
79	                labels[labels == self.tokenizer.pad_token_id] = -100
80	            batch["labels"] = labels
81	        return batch

290	def main():
291	    # See all possible arguments in src/transformers/training_args.py
292	    # or by passing the --help flag to this script.
293	    # We now keep distinct sets of args, for a cleaner separation of concerns.
294	
295	    parser = HfArgumentParser(
296	        (ModelArguments, DataTrainingArguments, TrainingArguments)
297	    )
298	    if len(sys.argv) == 2 and sys.argv[1].endswith(".json"):
299	        # If we pass only one argument to the script and it's the path to a json file,
300	        # let's parse it to get our arguments.
301	        model_args, data_args, training_args = parser.parse_json_file(
302	            json_file=os.path.abspath(sys.argv[1])
303	        )
304	    else:
305	        model_args, data_args, training_args = parser.parse_args_into_dataclasses()
306	
307	    # Sending telemetry. Tracking the example usage helps us better allocate resources to maintain them. The
308	    # information sent is the one passed as arguments along with your Python/PyTorch versions.
309	    send_example_telemetry("run_mlm", model_args, data_args)
310	
311	    # Setup logging
312	    logging.basicConfig(
313	        format="%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(name)s - %(message)s",
314	        datefmt="%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S",
315	        handlers=[logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)],
316	    )
317	
318	    log_level = training_args.get_process_log_level()
319	    logger.setLevel(log_level)
320	    datasets.utils.logging.set_verbosity(log_level)
321	    transformers.utils.logging.set_verbosity(log_level)
322	    transformers.utils.logging.enable_default_handler()
323	    transformers.utils.logging.enable_explicit_format()
324	
325	    # Log on each process the small summary:
326	    logger.warning(
327	        f"Process rank: {training_args.local_rank}, device: {training_args.device}, n_gpu: {training_args.n_gpu}"
328	        + f"distributed training: {bool(training_args.local_rank != -1)}, 16-bits training: {training_args.fp16}"
329	    )
330	    # Set the verbosity to info of the Transformers logger (on main process only):
331	    logger.info(f"Training/evaluation parameters {training_args}")
332	
333	    # Detecting last checkpoint.
334	    last_checkpoint = None
335	    if (
336	        os.path.isdir(training_args.output_dir)
337	        and training_args.do_train
338	        and not training_args.overwrite_output_dir
339	    ):
340	        last_checkpoint = get_last_checkpoint(training_args.output_dir)
341	        if last_checkpoint is None and len(os.listdir(training_args.output_dir)) > 0:
342	            raise ValueError(
343	                f"Output directory ({training_args.output_dir}) already exists and is not empty. "
344	                "Use --overwrite_output_dir to overcome."
345	            )
346	        elif (

363	        # streaming=True,

372	    config_kwargs = {

382	        )

384	        config = CONFIG_MAPPING[model_args.model_type]()
385	        logger.warning("You are instantiating a new config instance from scratch.")
386	        if model_args.config_overrides is not None:
387	            logger.info(f"Overriding config: {model_args.config_overrides}")
388	            config.update_from_string(model_args.config_overrides)

398	        tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(

401	    elif model_args.model_name_or_path:

404	        )

406	        raise ValueError(

413	            model_args.model_name_or_path,

425	    def tokenize_function(examples, soft_masked_weight):
426	        res = tokenizer(
427	            examples["seq"],
428	            return_special_tokens_mask=True,
429	            padding=False,
430	            truncation=False,
431	            return_token_type_ids=False,
432	            return_attention_mask=False,
433	        )
434	        res["loss_weights"] = np.ones_like(res["input_ids"], dtype=float)
435	        res["loss_weights"][
436	            np.char.islower([list(x) for x in examples["seq"]])
437	        ] = soft_masked_weight
438	        return res

440	    soft_masked_weight = {
441	        "train": data_args.soft_masked_loss_weights_train,
442	        "validation": data_args.soft_masked_loss_weights_evaluation,

470	            batched=True,

474	    data_collator = DataCollatorForLanguageModelingSimplified(
475	        tokenizer=tokenizer,
476	        mlm_probability=data_args.mlm_probability,
477	    )
478	
479	    # Initialize our Trainer
480	    trainer = Trainer(
481	        model=model,
482	        args=training_args,
483	        train_dataset=train_dataset if training_args.do_train else None,
484	        eval_dataset=eval_dataset if training_args.do_eval else None,
485	        tokenizer=tokenizer,

487	    )
488	
489	    # Training
490	    if training_args.do_train:
491	        checkpoint = None
492	        if training_args.resume_from_checkpoint is not None:
493	            checkpoint = training_args.resume_from_checkpoint
494	        elif last_checkpoint is not None:
495	            checkpoint = last_checkpoint
496	        train_result = trainer.train(resume_from_checkpoint=checkpoint)
497	        trainer.save_model()  # Saves the tokenizer too for easy upload
498	        metrics = train_result.metrics
499	
500	        trainer.log_metrics("train", metrics)
501	        trainer.save_metrics("train", metrics)
502	        trainer.save_state()
503	
504	    # Evaluation

506	        logger.info("*** Evaluate ***")
507	
508	        metrics = trainer.evaluate()
509	
510	        try:
511	            perplexity = math.exp(metrics["eval_loss"])
512	        except OverflowError:

514	        metrics["perplexity"] = perplexity
515	
516	        trainer.log_metrics("eval", metrics)
517	        trainer.save_metrics("eval", metrics)

519	    # Testing

526	        try:

535	    kwargs = {"finetuned_from": model_args.model_name_or_path, "tasks": "fill-mask"}
536	    if data_args.dataset_name is not None:

541	                "dataset"

1	import argparse, os
2	import numpy as np
3	import pandas as pd
4	import torch
5	from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
6	from transformers import AutoModelForMaskedLM, AutoTokenizer
7	from tqdm import tqdm
8	import xgboost as xgb
9	from sklearn import metrics
10	import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
11	import logging
12	
13	def parse_args():
14	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
15	    parser.add_argument("-train", type=str, help="The directory of training data")
16	    parser.add_argument("-valid", type=str, help="The directory of validation data")
17	    parser.add_argument("-test", type=str, help="The directory of test data")
18	    parser.add_argument("-model", type=str, help="The directory of pre-trained model")
19	    parser.add_argument("-output", type=str, help="The directory of output")
20	    parser.add_argument("-device", type=str, default="cuda:0", help="The device to run the model")
21	    parser.add_argument("-batchSize", type=int, default=128, help="The batch size for the model")
22	    parser.add_argument("-tokenIdx", type=int, default=255, help="The index of the nucleotide")
23	    parser.add_argument("-test_only", action='store_true', help="Flag to perform only testing")
24	    parser.add_argument("-save_memory", action='store_true', help="Flag to save memory, it only works for testing")
25	    parser.add_argument("-chunk_size", type=int, default=100000, help="The chunk size for testing, it only works for testing when save_memory is set")
26	    parser.add_argument("-seed", type=int, default=42, help="The random seed to train XGBoost model")
27	    return parser.parse_args()
28	
29	class SequenceDataset(Dataset):
30	    def __init__(self, sequences, tokenizer):
31	        self.sequences = sequences
32	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
33	
34	    def __len__(self):
35	        return len(self.sequences)
36	
37	    def __getitem__(self, idx):
38	        sequence = self.sequences[idx]
39	        encoding = self.tokenizer.encode_plus(
40	            sequence,
41	            return_tensors="pt",
42	            return_attention_mask=False,
43	            return_token_type_ids=False
44	        )
45	        input_ids = encoding['input_ids']
46	        return {
47	            'sequence': sequence,
48	            'input_ids': input_ids.squeeze()
49	        }
50	
51	def load_data(filepath):
52	    logging.info(f"Loading data from {filepath}")
53	    data = pd.read_csv(filepath, delimiter='\t')
54	    return data['sequences'].tolist(), data['label'].tolist()
55	
56	def create_dataloader(sequences, tokenizer, batch_size):
57	    logging.info(f"Creating DataLoader with batch size {batch_size}")
58	    dataset = SequenceDataset(sequences, tokenizer)
59	    return DataLoader(dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False)
60	
61	def load_model_and_tokenizer(model_dir, device):
62	    logging.info(f"Loading model and tokenizer from {model_dir}")
63	    
64	    # Determine the appropriate dtype based on the GPU capabilities
65	    def get_optimal_dtype():
66	        if not torch.cuda.is_available():
67	            logging.info("Using float32 as no GPU is available.")
68	            return torch.float32  
69	
70	        device_index = torch.cuda.current_device()
71	        capability = torch.cuda.get_device_capability(device_index)
72	
73	        if capability[0] >= 8:  # sm_80 or higher
74	            logging.info("Using bfloat16 as the GPU supports sm_80 or higher.")
75	            return torch.bfloat16
76	        elif capability[0] >= 6:  # sm_60 or higher
77	            logging.info("Using float16 as the GPU supports sm_60 or higher.")
78	            return torch.float16
79	        else:
80	            logging.info("Using float32 as the GPU does not support float16 or bfloat16.")
81	            return torch.float32
82	
83	    optimal_dtype = get_optimal_dtype()
84	
85	    # Load the model with the selected dtype
86	    try:
87	        model = AutoModelForMaskedLM.from_pretrained(model_dir, trust_remote_code=True, torch_dtype=optimal_dtype)
88	    except Exception as e:
89	        logging.error(f"Failed to load model with {optimal_dtype}, falling back to float32. Error: {e}")
90	        model = AutoModelForMaskedLM.from_pretrained(model_dir, trust_remote_code=True, torch_dtype=torch.float32)
91	
92	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_dir, trust_remote_code=True)
93	    model.to(device)
94	    return model, tokenizer
95	
96	def extract_embeddings(model, dataloader, device, tokenIdx):
97	    logging.info("Extracting embeddings")
98	    model.eval()
99	    embeddings = []
100	    with torch.no_grad():
101	        for batch in tqdm(dataloader):
102	            input_ids = batch['input_ids'].to(device)
103	            with torch.inference_mode():
104	                outputs = model(input_ids=input_ids, output_hidden_states=True)
105	            token_embedding = outputs.hidden_states[-1][:, tokenIdx, :].to(torch.float32).cpu().numpy()
106	            embeddings.append(token_embedding)
107	    embeddings = np.concatenate(embeddings, axis=0)
108	    # average forward and reverse embeddings
109	    hidden_size = embeddings.shape[-1] // 2
110	    forward = embeddings[..., 0:hidden_size]
111	    reverse = embeddings[..., hidden_size:]
112	    reverse = reverse[..., ::-1]
113	    averaged_embeddings = (forward + reverse) / 2
114	    return averaged_embeddings
115	
116	def train_xgboost_model(train_embeddings, train_labels, valid_embeddings, valid_labels, random_state=42):
117	    logging.info("Training XGBoost model")
118	    model = xgb.XGBClassifier(n_estimators=1000, max_depth=6, learning_rate=0.1, random_state=random_state, n_jobs=-1)
119	    model.fit(train_embeddings, train_labels, eval_set=[(valid_embeddings, valid_labels)])
120	    return model
121	
122	def infer_xgboost_model(model, embeddings):
123	    logging.info("Inferencing XGBoost model")
124	    return model.predict_proba(embeddings)[:, 1]
125	
126	def evaluate_model(predictions, labels):
127	    logging.info("Evaluating model")
128	    fpr, tpr, _ = metrics.roc_curve(labels, predictions)
129	    precision, recall, _ = metrics.precision_recall_curve(labels, predictions)
130	    roc_auc = metrics.auc(fpr, tpr)
131	    prauc = metrics.average_precision_score(labels, predictions)
132	    return fpr, tpr, precision, recall, roc_auc, prauc
133	
134	def plot_metrics(fpr, tpr, precision, recall, roc_auc, prauc, output_dir, prefix='valid', random_state=42):
135	    logging.info(f"Plotting metrics and saving to {output_dir}")
136	    fig, axs = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 6))
137	    axs[0].plot(fpr, tpr, label=f'AUC = {roc_auc:.2f}', linewidth=2)
138	    axs[0].set_title('ROC Curve')
139	    axs[0].set_xlabel('False Positive Rate')
140	    axs[0].set_ylabel('True Positive Rate')
141	    axs[0].legend(loc='lower right')
142	
143	    axs[1].plot(recall, precision, label=f'PRAUC = {prauc:.2f}', linewidth=2)
144	    axs[1].set_title('Precision-Recall Curve')
145	    axs[1].set_xlabel('Recall')
146	    axs[1].set_ylabel('Precision')
147	    axs[1].legend(loc='lower left')
148	
149	    plt.tight_layout()
150	    plt.savefig(os.path.join(output_dir, f'seed_{random_state}_{prefix}_metrics.png'))
151	    # save metrics to file
152	    with open(os.path.join(output_dir, f'seed_{random_state}_{prefix}_metrics.txt'), 'w') as f:
153	        f.write(f"ROC AUC: {roc_auc:.2f}\n")
154	        f.write(f"PRAUC: {prauc:.2f}\n")
155

157	def main():
158	    logging.basicConfig(
159	        level=logging.INFO,
160	        format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s',
161	        datefmt='%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'
162	    )
163	    args = parse_args()
164	    os.makedirs(args.output, exist_ok=True)
165	
166	    model, tokenizer = load_model_and_tokenizer(args.model, args.device)
167	
168	    if args.test_only:
169	        if args.test:
170	            test_sequences, test_labels = load_data(args.test)
171	            xgb_model = xgb.XGBClassifier()

173	            prefix = os.path.basename(args.test).split('.')[0]

175	            if args.save_memory: # split the test data into smaller chunks
176	                logging.info("Saving memory by splitting the test data into smaller chunks with size {}".format(args.chunk_size))
177	                predictions = []
178	                for i in range(0, len(test_sequences), args.chunk_size):
179	                    if os.path.exists(os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_chunk_{i}_embeddings.npz')):

186	                        np.savez_compressed(os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_chunk_{i}_embeddings.npz'), test=test_embeddings)
187	                    pred = infer_xgboost_model(xgb_model, test_embeddings)

191	            else:

194	                    embeddings = np.load(os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_embeddings.npz'))

211	        train_loader = create_dataloader(train_sequences, tokenizer, args.batchSize)

213	        if os.path.exists(os.path.join(args.output, 'train_valid_embeddings.npz')):

267	                predictions = infer_xgboost_model(xgb_model, test_embeddings)
268	                    
269	            np.savez_compressed(os.path.join(args.output, f'seed_{args.seed}_{prefix}_predictions.npz'), predictions=predictions)
270	            fpr, tpr, precision, recall, roc_auc, prauc = evaluate_model(predictions, test_labels)
271	            prefix = os.path.basename(args.test).split('.')[0]
272	            plot_metrics(fpr, tpr, precision, recall, roc_auc, prauc, args.output, prefix=prefix, random_state=args.seed)
273	
274	if __name__ == "__main__":
275	    main()

1	import argparse, os, sys
2	sys.path.append(os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__)))
3	from train_XGBoost import *
4	import logging
5	
6	def parse_args():
7	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
8	    parser.add_argument("-test", type=str, help="The directory of test data")
9	    parser.add_argument("-model", type=str, help="The directory of pre-trained model")
10	    parser.add_argument("-classifier", type=str, help="The directory of trained XGBoost models")
11	    parser.add_argument("-output", type=str, help="The directory of output")
12	    parser.add_argument("-device", type=str, default="cuda:0", help="The device to run the model")
13	    parser.add_argument("-batchSize", type=int, default=128, help="The batch size for the model")
14	    parser.add_argument("-tokenIdx", type=int, default=255, help="The index of the nucleotide")
15	    parser.add_argument("-save_memory", action='store_true', help="Flag to save memory, it only works for testing")
16	    parser.add_argument("-chunk_size", type=int, default=100000, help="The chunk size for testing, it only works for testing when save_memory is set")
17	    return parser.parse_args()
18	
19	def main():
20	    logging.basicConfig(
21	        level=logging.INFO,
22	        format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s',
23	        datefmt='%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'
24	    )
25	    args = parse_args()
26	    os.makedirs(args.output, exist_ok=True)
27	
28	    model, tokenizer = load_model_and_tokenizer(args.model, args.device)
29	
30	    test_sequences, test_labels = load_data(args.test)
31	    xgb_model = xgb.XGBClassifier()
32	    xgb_model.load_model(args.classifier)
33	    prefix = os.path.basename(args.test).split('.')[0]
34	
35	    if args.save_memory: # split the test data into smaller chunks
36	        logging.info("Saving memory by splitting the test data into smaller chunks with size {}".format(args.chunk_size))
37	        predictions = []
38	        for i in range(0, len(test_sequences), args.chunk_size):
39	            if os.path.exists(os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_chunk_{i}_embeddings.npz')):
40	                logging.info(f"Found pre-computed embeddings, loading from file {os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_chunk_{i}_embeddings.npz')}")
41	                embeddings = np.load(os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_chunk_{i}_embeddings.npz'))
42	                test_embeddings = embeddings['test']
43	            else:
44	                test_loader = create_dataloader(test_sequences[i:i+args.chunk_size], tokenizer, args.batchSize)
45	                test_embeddings = extract_embeddings(model, test_loader, args.device, args.tokenIdx)
46	                np.savez_compressed(os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_chunk_{i}_embeddings.npz'), test=test_embeddings)
47	            pred = infer_xgboost_model(xgb_model, test_embeddings)
48	            pred = pred[:, np.newaxis]
49	            predictions.extend(pred)
50	        predictions = np.concatenate(predictions, axis=0)
51	    else:
52	        if os.path.exists(os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_embeddings.npz')):
53	            logging.info(f"Found pre-computed embeddings, loading from file {os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_embeddings.npz')}")
54	            embeddings = np.load(os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_embeddings.npz'))
55	            test_embeddings = embeddings['test']
56	        else:
57	            test_loader = create_dataloader(test_sequences, tokenizer, args.batchSize)
58	            test_embeddings = extract_embeddings(model, test_loader, args.device, args.tokenIdx)
59	            np.savez_compressed(os.path.join(args.output, prefix + '_embeddings.npz'), test=test_embeddings)
60	        predictions = infer_xgboost_model(xgb_model, test_embeddings)
61	
62	    output_df = pd.DataFrame({
63	        'label': test_labels,
64	        'prediction': predictions
65	    })
66	    output_df.to_csv(os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_predictions.tsv'), sep='\t', index=False)
67	    logging.info(f"Saved predictions to {os.path.join(args.output, f'{prefix}_predictions.tsv')}")
68	
69	if __name__ == "__main__":
70	    main()
